title: micropterum longipes
intro: Micropterum longipes, also known as the hairy beggar ticks or hairy beggar-ticks, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion tribe within the sunflower family. It is a weed found in various parts of the world, particularly in tropical and subtropical regions. The plant has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Micropterum longipes has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, and can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture for herbal consumption.
